text.title: Gcp Overview Cli Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for the gcp-overview-cli methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale + concrete remediation hook.

rule.statement: Local development MUST use `gcloud auth application-default login` (or `auth login --impersonate-service-account`); downloaded SA JSON keys are forbidden on dev machines.
rule.rationale: ADC + impersonation produce short-lived, auditable tokens; SA JSON keys on laptops are a routine breach source.

rule.statement: Engineers working across multiple projects MUST create per-env `gcloud config configurations` rather than re-`set`-ing project per command.
rule.rationale: Sticky project state causes commands to land in the wrong project; named configurations make environment switches explicit.

rule.statement: CI/CD and ops tasks MUST use `--impersonate-service-account=...` instead of activating a SA key file.
rule.rationale: Impersonation is auditable and revocable; activating a key file makes the runner the SA persistently.

rule.statement: Commands containing secrets MUST be invoked with `set -o histignorespace` (or via files), not pasted into shell history.
rule.rationale: Shell history is routinely synced to dotfiles repos and synced drives; secrets in history leak.

rule.statement: Commands targeting regional/zonal resources MUST pass `--region` / `--zone` explicitly rather than relying on `gcloud config`.
rule.rationale: Implicit region/zone lands commands in the wrong location during context switches; explicit flags make the target obvious.

rule.statement: If the input fails ALL "Applies If" preconditions or matches ANY "Skip If" disqualifier, do not apply this methodology — route the task to the adjacent sibling instead.
rule.rationale: Even flat checklists need an explicit skip leaf in the decision tree; this rule is the universal terminal.
